channel.title: git on Greg Beifuss
channel.description: Recent content in git on Greg Beifuss

item.title: Pushing to GitHub via. Git
item.description: When I first committed to actually publishing a blog and settled on using Hugo and Github, I ran into a problem when trying to actually synchronize my local repository with Github: I kept getting a ‘Permission Denied (publickey)’ error when I pushed the content up to Github. After some Google-fu, I found that the error was because I didn’t have any SSH keys setup on my Windows 10 system and it couldn’t communicate securely with GitHub.

item.title: Pushing Hugo Content to GitHub
item.description: This blog content is generated by the Hugo static site generator and integrated with GitHub (which you can clearly tell by the URL), but I’m always struggling to remember the steps in pushing the newest content publicly. Change to your site’s local directory, then tell hugo to build the content: cd myblog.dev.repo hugo Change to your site’s public directory (where the new content was just generated):
